subject: Product Management
title: Google Search
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Google Search.

hook.text: In 1998, there were already 18 search engines. Google launched with a blank page and one text box. And won.

buildup.text: Larry Page and Sergey Brin's insight: rank pages by how many other pages link to them, not just by keywords.

discovery.text: PageRank treated links like academic citations — a page linked by many trusted sources must be important.

twist.text: The product decision to keep the homepage empty was radical. Competitors crammed portals with news, ads, and links.

climax.text: Google's clean homepage communicated a promise: we do one thing, and we do it better than anyone.

punchline.text: One text box beat portals with everything. Simplicity won.

quiz.question: What product decisions made Google Search succeed?
options: PageRank algorithm and a radically simple homepage
Having the most features of any search engine
Spending more on advertising than competitors
